title: Work-order condition status colors
definition: Native DF renders an existing item condition's next-check status label and text attributes from its cached satisfaction result.

facts.statement: A true cached result is labeled Satisfied for next check in bright native green foreground index 2 on black background index 0.
A false cached result is labeled Not satisfied for next check in bright native red foreground index 4 on black background index 0.
These attributes apply to the status annotation at the right of an existing item condition, not to its operator, quantity, or item-description fragments.

uncertainties.statement: The corpus confirms the false-state wording and colors, but the same condition has not yet been recorded in the true cached state.

positive: The same existing item-condition row with a true cached result shows the exact satisfied text and bright green-on-black attributes.
boundary: Foreground, background, and brightness planes are all checked rather than inferring color from visible text.
counterexamples: The same row with a false cached result shows the exact unsatisfied text and bright red-on-black attributes.

absenceGuards: Do not apply the status annotation color to the operator, quantity, or item-description fragments.

requiredFrames: The same condition row in satisfied and unsatisfied next-check states
